stories.title: Senate votes to fund most of DHS. And, Trump extends Iran's deadline to reopen strait
stories.description: The Senate has voted to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security. And, President Trump extends the deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: A month of war has destroyed a third of Iran's missiles, per U.S. intelligence
stories.description: The Trump administration says it's ahead of schedule in destroying Iran's weapons, but NPR has learned that a U.S. intelligence assessment says about a third of Iran's missiles have been destroyed.

stories.title: Rubio meets with other G7 nations for first time since Iran war
stories.description: Secretary of State Rubio meets his G7 colleagues in Paris for the first time since the start of the war on Iran.

stories.title: Southeast Asia is being hit hard by Iran's cutoff of oil and gas
stories.description: Southeast Asia is among the areas hardest hit by Iran's cutoff of oil and gas through the Strait of Hormuz, with many nations almost entirely dependent on foreign energy — and quickly running out.

stories.title: Iran's war propaganda includes memes directed at Trump
stories.description: Iran has been sending not only missiles around the region but also trolling tweets and videos around the internet. It's the latest in global diplomacy.

stories.title: The U.S. and Iran can't even agree on whether they're talking
stories.description: President Trump says Iran is "begging" for a deal. Iran says it has no intention of negotiating. NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with NPR's Tom Bowman and Aya Batrawy about prospects for an off-ramp.

stories.title: Is Trump's pause on attacking Iranian energy for diplomacy or an escalation?
stories.description: The US president's commitment to deadlines is fluid but he uses them for a purpose, writes the BBC's James Landale.

stories.title: UN human rights chief calls on US to conclude probe into Iran school strike
stories.description: The strike - which killed at least 168 people, mostly children - "evoked a visceral horror", Volker Türk said.

stories.title: How the Hormuz closure could affect food, medicines and smartphones
stories.description: The price of a wide range of goods - from food, to smartphones, to medicines - could be affected by the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.title: Palestinian man killed as death toll from West Bank settler violence climbs
stories.description: Mohammad al-Malhi is the seventh Palestinian killed by a settler in a surge of violence since the start of the war with Iran.

stories.title: Trump's Iran strategy is to pursue two off-ramps at once
stories.description: The White House insists the president is dictating events, but even some Republicans are frustrated by mixed messaging about his war aims.

stories.title: Ukraine signs deal with Saudi Arabia offering drone expertise
stories.description: Volodymyr Zelensky says the Gulf kingdom faces the same type of attacks from Iran that Kyiv has been resisting.

stories.title: Israel says it has killed Iran's navy chief overseeing Strait of Hormuz blockade
stories.description: Israel's defence minister said a number of other "senior Navy command officials" have also been killed. Iran has not yet commented.

stories.title: Iran's rejection of US talks reflects deep mistrust
stories.description: This does not mean the door is shut, as Iran's tough stance may be more about setting conditions than rejecting diplomacy.

stories.title: Iran's foreign minister says there are no negotiations with US
stories.description: Trump says he is talking to "the right people" in Iran, but Tehran says no such negotiations are being held.

stories.title: 'There's no safety anymore': Palestinians warn of expanding West Bank settler violence
stories.description: There has been a surge of attacks by Israeli settlers on Palestinian villages across the occupied territory since the start of the Iran war.

stories.title: Small window open for US-Iran talks, but swift end to war still unlikely
stories.description: President Trump says the US is already dealing with a "top person" in Iran, but Tehran denies that any talks have begun.

stories.title: What Iranians make of the possibility of talks to end the war
stories.description: Some Iranians dismiss the possibility of talks to end the war, others worry a deal may leave the current leaders in place.

stories.title: A committed pharmacist and a homesick blogger – the Iranian civilians killed in the war
stories.description: The toll on civilians is mounting fast as the US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

stories.title: US' and Iran's options for ending war narrow the longer it goes on
stories.description: Iran shows no sign of climbdown, while air power alone cannot achieve the US' main goal.

stories.title: Trump at a crossroads as US weighs tough options in Iran
stories.description: Statements from President Trump that the war is almost over are not matching up with the reality on the ground.

stories.title: Rubio says Iran war to last weeks, no US ground troops needed
stories.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Friday that Washington expects its military operation against Iran to wrap up within weeks without the need for ground troops. This comes as G7 nations adopted a declaration on Iran calling for an immediate halt to attacks against civilian p...

stories.title: Egypt imposes business curfew to counter soaring fuel costs
stories.description: In tonight's edition, Egypt orders shops, restaurants and shopping malls to shut their doors from 9:00 pm Saturday as it hopes to cushion the blow of energy prices that have more than doubled because of the Iran war. Also, rising jihadist violence in the Sahel is hitting tourism ...

stories.title: War in Lebanon 'was imposed upon us', PM Nawaf Salam says
stories.description: In an interview with FRANCE 24 in Beirut, Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam discussed the Israeli offensive in Lebanon, amid the ongoing war in the Middle East. "This war was imposed upon us," the Lebanese leader said, adding that Lebanon "could have avoided it" had Hezbollah n...

stories.title: Iran: Rolling Ultimatums, Moscow "at the EU table"?
stories.description: In a week of movable ultimatums set by President Trump, to “obliterate Iran’s power plants”, a deadline was set initially of Monday at Midnight, then extended by five days on account of “very productive conversations”, then to ten days with Trump claiming “talks are ongoing” and ...

stories.title: France rebukes Lavrov, says Russia does not defend international law
stories.description: France’s foreign minister on Friday accused Russia of flouting international law in both Ukraine and Iran, rebuking remarks by his Russian counterpart Sergei Lavrov after he defended Moscow’s stance in a French television interview.

stories.title: At G7, France hits back at Lavrov, says Russia does not defend international law
stories.description: France's Foreign ​Minister Jean-Noel Barrot said on ​Friday that Russia does not defend international law either in Ukraine ​or ‌Iran with its ⁠actions, in response to comments made ‌by his Russian counterpart Sergei Lavrov ⁠in an interview on French TV. "Mr. Lavrov was ​able to ...

stories.title: Why Pakistan has emerged as a mediator between US and Iran
stories.description: As fears of a wider regional conflict escalate following US and Israeli strikes on Iran that began in late February, Pakistan has emerged as an unexpected mediator, offering to help bring Washington and Tehran to the negotiating table. Islamabad isn't often called on to act as an...

stories.title: Trump administration sidelines, threatens Europe in Iran war: EU flounders in new world order
stories.description: After Greenland and trade tariffs, US President Donald Trump is threatening European countries once again. If they fail to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz, "it will be very bad for the future of NATO", Trump has said. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio has made it clear that the ...

stories.title: US, Israeli war goals 'not realistic': Regime change in Iran 'can't be achieved' by airstrikes
stories.description: William Hilderbrandt is pleased to welcome Nitzan Horowitz, Journalist and former Israeli Minister of Health (2021–2022). According to Horowitz, regime change and the dismantling of military capabilities were unattainable. And now there's a widening gap emerging between expectati...

stories.title: G7 allies press Rubio on US Iran plans, discuss Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: G7 allies were on Friday pressing US Secretary of State Marco Rubio for clarity on American plans for Iran almost one month into the war in the Middle East, with concern also intensifying over the extent of cooperation between Russia and the Islamic republic. Before leaving for F...

stories.title: Pakistan the peacemaker? Islamabad steps up as US-Iran mediator
stories.description: On this week's Access Asia we look at how Pakistan has emerged as a key mediator to end the war in Iran. The Atlantic Council's Michael Kugelman tells us the conflict spillover risks are particularly acute, adding that Pakistan badly wants to avoid getting dragged into the war.

stories.title: Rescue workers rush to save lives after south Tehran missile strike
stories.description: Iranian rescue workers have been pulling survivors and bodies out of a building after a missile strike on south Tehran.

stories.title: Houthis warn ‘fingers on the trigger’ as US-Israel war on Iran continues
stories.description: The Iran-aligned Yemeni group have the ability to target key shipping lanes around the Arabian Peninsula.

stories.title: ‘Fingers on the trigger’ for military intervention, Houthis warn
stories.description: The Houthis have warned that they are ready for direct military intervention in the US-Israeli war on Iran.

stories.title: What is the TACO trade and are investors profiting amid war on Iran?
stories.description: Global energy crisis continues to widen amid market volatility, while so-called 'TACO traders' attempt to capitalise.

stories.title: Guns in the streets as US, Israel intensify month-long attacks across Iran
stories.description: A senior IRGC official in Tehran told state television that children over 12 can join armed patrols and checkpoints.

stories.title: Grieving Iranian mother tells UN about children before school attack
stories.description: An Iranian mother told the UN Human Right’s Council about the last time she saw her children alive.

stories.title: Israel launches strikes on nuclear sites as Iran warns of retaliation
stories.description: Uranium facility, steel plants and heavy water complex among targets hit as IRGC warns of escalation.

stories.title: How important are the Gulf economies to the world?
stories.description: Impact of US-Israeli war on Iran poses serious risks beyond energy sector.

stories.title: Israel attacks major industrial and nuclear sites in Iran
stories.description: Smoke was seen rising from Iran’s Arak heavy-water plant following Israeli air strikes on key infrastructure.

stories.title: Drone reveals extent of Iranian missile damage in Israeli city of Arad
stories.description: Drone footage has revealed the extent of damage in Arad days after an Iranian missile attack.

stories.title: Smoke rises over Beirut suburbs after massive Israeli airstrike
stories.description: Smoke rose over the skyline of Beirut following airstrikes as Israel continued to escalate its war on Hezbollah.

stories.title: Rubio: US expects Iran war to end in ‘weeks, not months’
stories.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the US expects its military operation in Iran to conclude in weeks, not months.

stories.title: US diplomat Marco Rubio denounces settler violence, tolls in Hormuz strait
stories.description: Rubio reiterated President Donald Trump's call for other countries to participate in securing the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: How close is the US to a quagmire in Iran?
stories.description: The US has a 15-point plan to end the war on Iran, but what does the military reality show?

stories.title: Iran’s football team honours children killed in Minab school airstrike
stories.description: Iranian football players laid backpacks on a football pitch in Turkey to pay tribute to the children killed in a US-Isra

stories.title: Part of Iranian missile falls on complex housing TASS bureau in Israel
stories.description: No casualties were reported

stories.title: Iran notifies of another strike on Bushehr nuke plant area — IAEA
stories.description: No damage to the reactor or radiation release was reported

stories.title: Israel, US attack Bushehr NPP area for third time — Atomic Energy Organization of Iran
stories.description: There were no reports of casualties, damage or technical failures

stories.title: Russia points to nuclear safety threats from strikes near Bushehr plant in UN
stories.description: The Russian delegation also cited data on casualties among civilians, destruction of civilian infrastructure, and attacks on educational institutions

stories.title: Iran to facilitate passage of humanitarian cargoes via Strait of Hormuz at UN request
stories.description: Permanent Representative to the UN Geneva office Ali Bahreini says that all necessary arrangements will be finalized "in due course"

stories.title: US may change sanctions against Russian oil — Rubio
stories.description: The US secretary of state says that the president did not express desire to lift any sanctions permanently

stories.title: Duma deputies temporarily relieved of sanctions before US visit — deputy
stories.description: Mikhail Delyagin says that the goal of the deputies was to show that the sanctions "harm not so much Russia as the United States itself"

stories.title: Trump bored with military operation against Iran — MS Now
stories.description: According to the source quoted by the TV channel, the US president "wants to declare victory and move on"

stories.title: US tells allies ground op in Iran not imminent — Bloomberg
stories.description: Sources cited by the news agency admit that US President Donald Trump could change his mind and order a ground invasion

stories.title: At least 13 people killed, over 50 injured in US-Israeli attacks on Iran’s Kermanshah
stories.description: The attacks targeted residential quarters in the city of Kermanshah and a neighborhood in the province’s eastern part

stories.title: Russia doing nothing that might impede US operation against Iran — Rubio
stories.description: Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov said earlier that Russia had provided no intelligence to Iran in its confrontations with the United States and Israel

stories.title: Israel attacks heavy water plant in Iran’s Arak — IDF
stories.description: Heavy water is a material used to operate nuclear reactors

stories.title: Hezbollah aims to kill as many Israeli troops as possible — expert
stories.description: As Hezbollah is well-versed in the terrain, it has the upper hand in carrying out ambush attacks, Lebanese General Hassan Juni noted
